title: C++ Input (cin)
content:
### Console Input with cin

The `cin` object, combined with the extraction operator `>>`, reads input from the keyboard.

---

### Basic Input

```cpp
int age;
cout << "Enter your age: ";
cin >> age;
```

---

### Multiple Inputs

```cpp
int x, y;
cin >> x >> y;  // Reads two space-separated integers
```

---

### Input with Strings

```cpp
string name;
cin >> name;                // Reads one word (stops at space)

getline(cin, name);         // Reads entire line
```

---

### Input Validation

| Check | Code | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| Success | `if (cin >> x)` | Returns true if input succeeded |
| Failure | `cin.fail()` | True if last input failed |
| Clear | `cin.clear()` | Reset error state |
| Ignore | `cin.ignore(100, '\\n')` | Discard up to 100 chars or until newline |

---

### Mixed Input Gotcha

```cpp
int age;
string name;

cin >> age;                    // Leaves newline in buffer
cin.ignore();                  // Must ignore newline
getline(cin, name);            // Now works correctly
```

---

> **Key Point:** When mixing `cin >>` and `getline()`, use `cin.ignore()` to consume the newline left by the extraction operator.

quiz.question: Which operator is used with cin to read input?
